name: Bank API
what_it_does: A design reference implementation for building compliant, modern financial REST APIs using ASP.NET Core 10, including an MCP server layer that exposes API tools to AI assistants like Claude and GitHub Copilot.
use_cases: Bootstrap a new compliant financial services API following OWASP, GDPR, and Dutch Public Sector REST Design Rules
Integrate a bank/financial API with AI assistants via the included MCP server
Learn best practices for API versioning, security, and observability in .NET
not_for: Production banking systems without significant customization — this is a reference/template, not a production-ready product
Non-.NET stacks — the implementation is ASP.NET Core specific
Teams that need a simple, minimal MCP server without compliance overhead
best_when: You are bootstrapping a .NET financial API and want compliance (OWASP, GDPR, OpenAPI 3.1) and AI integration baked in from day one.
avoid_when: You need a language-agnostic or production-hardened banking platform rather than a reference template.

auth.notes: Supports API Key, JWT Bearer, and OpenID Connect (Entra ID). The MCP server supports API Key and OAuth Authorization Code flows. Token reuse prevention implemented.

pricing.notes: MIT-compatible (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0). Live demo hosted on Azure F1 tier.

compliance: Dutch Public Sector REST API Design Rules

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Generic exception handling is explicitly noted as minimally implemented in the README — a known gap.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Not explicitly documented; REST design rules likely encourage it.
known_agent_gotchas: Scalar does not fully support OpenID Connect workflows — may confuse agents trying to authenticate
Dual stable/beta versioning adds complexity for agents selecting the right endpoint
Live demo hosted on F1 tier Azure App Service — expect cold starts and limited throughput

security_score_components.security_notes: Banking API MCP — extremely sensitive financial data. OAuth2 with strong scopes mandatory. PSD2/Open Banking standards. Audit every agent action. Use read-only scopes unless writes required.
